articles.title: Zig: Build System Reworked
articles.summary: Zig's build system has been reworked to separate the maker process from the configurer process, allowing for faster compilation and execution. The new approach involves compiling build.zig files into a small process in debug mode, serializing it to a binary configuration file, and then asynchronously compiling the maker process in release mode. This change enables features such as --watch, --fuzz, and --webui without increasing build time, and optimizes the build graph execution process for improved performance.

articles.title: What Is a Dickover?
articles.summary: A Dickover is a modal panel or popover presented by a website or app to frustrate users with unwanted interactions, such as asking for cookies, subscription information, or mobile app installation. These intrusive elements are often used to manipulate user behavior and can be found in various online platforms, including blogs, news sites, and social media apps. They can be considered a form of web abuse, spreading a malicious design pattern that has become ubiquitous on the internet.

articles.title: CVE-2026-48710: A Maintainer's Perspective
articles.summary: The CVE-2026-48710 vulnerability affects applications using Starlette's routing feature, which incorrectly reconstructs the request URL by concatenating the host with the path. This can lead to unauthorized access if a middleware uses the reconstructed URL to guard a route. The issue arises from an application pattern built on top of Starlette that assumes the request's path and host are always correct, whereas in reality they may be malformed or different from what was expected.

articles.title: NNN Stack: NixOS, Niri, Noctalia
articles.summary: The NixOS and Niri stacks offer declarative scrollable Wayland-native frameworks that provide a flexible and reproducible desktop experience. The NixOS stack, built on top of the Linux kernel, enables developers to create entire systems as code, with rollbacks possible down to the last byte. In contrast, the Niri stack offers a compositor-based approach, allowing for scrollable tiling and a cohesive user interface. By leveraging these technologies, developers can build more robust and maintainable applications that are easier to reproduce on different hardware configurations.

articles.title: bijou64: A variable-length integer encoding
articles.summary: This small encoding called bijou64 is a variable-length integer (varint) designed for the Subduction CRDT sync protocol, intended to fix a signature-verification bug by making each number only representable in one way. It was found that this design constraint made it run faster than other common varint designs like LEB128. The problem with bijou64 is its ability to encode numbers as sequences of 7-bit segments, allowing for efficient representation even when numbers are small but occasionally large. This practical solution has the potential to be useful in various applications where compact encoding is necessary, despite its limitations and potential issues with canonicalization and signature verification.
